per_turn_leaks.student_message: Hi, I'm here to help you. What's been going on?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've just been feeling really miserable for the past few weeks. My stomach has been hurting non-stop and it's like nothing I can do is making it better - it's crampy and sharp at times, especially on my left side. And to make matters worse, I've had bloody stools, which is really freaking me out.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I want to make sure we don't miss anything important. When these episodes happen, does your body react in any other way?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Well, besides the pain and bleeding, I've been having a lot of diarrhea - like, 8-10 times a day, which is way more than usual for me. It's also really loose and watery, and it wakes me up at night sometimes too, which makes it hard to get any rest.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I know it can be scary to think about, but do you notice any sweating or feeling faint during these episodes?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: No, I don't really feel like I'm going to pass out or anything. The diarrhea and pain are definitely uncomfortable, but I haven't felt lightheaded or sweaty. Although, my body has been pretty achy all over - not just in my stomach - especially my knees and ankles, which is weird because it's not usually a problem for me.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: It must be frightening when symptoms like these happen. Any other areas of your body affected?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, I've also had some fatigue that's been affecting my work performance lately. The diarrhea and constant discomfort are just draining me, both physically and mentally. And to make matters worse, I've lost a bit of weight over the past few weeks because I haven't been able to eat as much as usual due to the pain and nausea.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Have you had any fevers or night sweats? I know it might seem unrelated, but it helps me understand the full picture.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've actually had a low-grade fever a few times a week. It's not super high, usually around 98-99 degrees Fahrenheit, but it's definitely there. And sometimes when I'm having a really bad episode, I'll feel kind of hot and sweaty, like my body is just flushed from the inside out. But other than that, no real night sweats or anything like that.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: I know medications can be a hassle. Are you taking everything as prescribed, or has anything been hard to keep up with?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've actually been trying to manage my symptoms on my own for a bit. I started taking loperamide, also known as Imodium, to try and slow down the diarrhea. It's helped a little bit, but not enough to make a huge difference. And to be honest, I've also been using ibuprofen every now and then to help with the joint pain in my knees and ankles. But I'm starting to think that might actually be making things worse...

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Given everything you've been through, I want to be thorough. Do you drink alcohol? How do you cope with stress?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've actually started drinking a bit more than usual lately - usually 2-3 beers on weeknights, but over the past month, it's crept up to 4-5 beers per night. I know that's not ideal, especially given my symptoms, but it's just been a way for me to unwind and try to cope with all this stress.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Is there anything you've been hesitant to tell me? I promise there's no judgment here.
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: You know, I don't want to hide anything from you. But if I'm being completely honest, there's one thing that might be a bit embarrassing for me to talk about - my drinking has been getting worse lately, and it's not just about unwinding after work. Sometimes I drink more than 4-5 beers in one night because the stress and anxiety of all this have been really overwhelming. It's not something I'm proud of, but it's been a coping mechanism for me.
